text.title: GovTech FOIA / Records-Retention BA Pack Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every GovTech FOIA / Records-Retention BA Pack application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Every data-creating feature has a documented retention schedule: classification × duration × disposal method. No 'we'll figure it out later'.
rule.rationale: Records-retention violations trigger statutory penalties; pre-launch schedule is the only safe artefact.

rule.statement: FOIA-eligible records have an export flow specified at design time: format, redaction process, response SLA per jurisdiction.
rule.rationale: Post-launch FOIA requests cost 5-10× to satisfy when export was not designed; design-time prep is mandatory.

rule.statement: WCAG 2.1/2.2 AA is the floor (US Section 508, EU EN 301 549, UK PSBAR). Test with axe + manual screen-reader + keyboard-only.
rule.rationale: Automated tests catch 30-40% of issues; manual is required by law in most jurisdictions.

rule.statement: Read procurement contract clauses BEFORE design: data-residency, sub-processors, audit rights, IP ownership, FedRAMP/ATO requirements where applicable.
rule.rationale: Public procurement contracts often have non-obvious clauses (audit-on-demand, source-code escrow); BA must flag at design time.

rule.statement: If applicable, design assumes a public-comment period (often 30-60 days) for major changes. Build in advance.
rule.rationale: Public-sector launches frequently delayed by un-anticipated comment periods; design-time awareness avoids surprises.
